reviews.title: Great Choice!
reviews.text: Does beauty count?  This computer is sexy!  RGB lighting is so pretty.  Im not a techy person so all I can say is that I have been playing ESO and WOW all day and night over the weekend.  This machine is a BEAST!  It purs and it has never over heated, gliched or lagged.  Best choice I made!
reviews.author: Clwinchell

reviews.title: Never buy a cyperpowerpc!
reviews.text: Will never buy a cyperpower PC again. Poor customer support for a computer that broke in about 10 months under warranty. Spent about 2 weeks messaging CyberPowerPC just for them to want me to ship the computer back on my own dime (UPS quoted around $300 with insurance). The part that needs to be replaced is 80-100$. Would have been happy if they just sent the part, but now I'm about to go buy the part myself.
reviews.author: AmberW

reviews.title: Nightmare
reviews.text: I had to return my first one because i kept getting a random blacked out monitor while my vga light flickered red my keyboard and mouse would shut off but the tower would still be on and once my monitors turn back on again all my stuff is closed as if it restarted. So i returned it and got the same one again thinking maybe something was wrong with the first one NOPE the SECOND one i got had the same EXACT issue.
reviews.author: Selena

reviews.title: New Birthday PC
reviews.text: Got this as a birthday gift. Upgraded from a 2nd gen/ddr3 ram build. The wire management is great and was ready to go right out of the box without any issues. Highly recommended for anyone looking at an affordable new build that will keep up with every game out there
reviews.author: ReinaldoG

reviews.title: My Dream Pc
reviews.text:
I've always wanted to own any desktop with a 30 series GPU, as I was finally able to. This desktop would be really terrific for my 1440p gaming and even 4k gaming. With this GPU I will be able to run every game i play at 1440p on ultra settings with a stunning 120 fps+.
Thank you Cyberpower for achieving my dream pc.

reviews.title: Love this PC!
reviews.text: I purchased this PC to use with my interior design software and LOVE it! The processing speed is very fast and I love the keyboard and mouse that computer came with!

reviews.text: Awesome and powerful computer.  I work on video and audio projects with multiple screens with no problem. Video streaming and internet speeds are fast. Runs beautifully.

reviews.title: Problems out of box
reviews.text: $2100 pc and problems on first day. Pc boots up and will run then randomly lose signal to monitor. The only way to restore signal is to restart pc. Constantly losing signal. This is why I bought a prebuilt, to avoid issues. Huge mistake.

reviews.title: Good buy
reviews.text: PC came as promised was a little late but no biggie. Didnt have an issue turning on or hooking up to monitors. Performance great so far havent had any issues.

reviews.title: Overheated
reviews.text: As a price perform way it is really nice but its overheated while you play Battlefield 2042. Already replaced ones because its cpu burnt. Add cooler in last purchase and its ok now.
reviews.author: EceF

reviews.title: Stay away unless you're ready to put in some work
reviews.text:
There are good things about this computer, but there are many more things that are bad.

The good:
It is aesthetically very pleasing. The RGB components seem to have good quality LEDs and the cables seem to be managed well (in the visible areas). The case is also pleasant to look at.
It is also packaged very well. It shipped in the case box and packed with soft foam. It also has the hardening foam inside the case to keep the gfx card from being too stressed during shipping.
Choice of components seem to be good (on the surface) when it comes to expanability.

The bad:
Out of the box this PC is not a great experience to use. Once you get loaded into Windows 11, you're immediately met with popups from the pre-installed gigabyte software for the motherboard. Pre-installed software isn't the issue, it's the fact that it spams you until you interact. A mistake I made was interacting with these. One piece of software called control center offered the ability to update your drivers with one click. Nice! Well, this led to instability and blue screens post updates. Not a problem, I'll just re-install windows from the local recovery partition. Well now my USB ports frequently disconnects. Sometimes they'll reconnect after a few minutes, other times I have to hard reset my computer to get functionality back. And if you don't hard reset, the USB ports still won't work even in bios which means it's an issue with the USB controller on the mobo or the chipset. Alright let me flash my bios with the most current stable release. Still issues. Let me install a 30 dollar pcie USB card. That fixed it. The USB controller on the MOBO is just easily overwhelmed on a computer that's work more than some cards. Great. Next I need to install some software that's only available from the windows store. No windows store. This might be a plus for some people, but not for me. Some software like iCloud and EarTrumpet are only available on windows store. Okay now let me try to re-install the windows store. Powershell script you'd normally use doesn't work. Built in store troubleshooter doesn't work. Restarting the windows service for installing the store, doesn't work. The only way I found to install the store was to go into the Xbox app, goto settings and they said required services for this app to were missing. From there I could install it when admin powershell wouldn't. for some reason. So now I have the windows store. cool. So let's review what I had to do to make this computer stable and able to be usable for daily uses for myself.
Uninstall gigabyte software that would auto update to unstable drivers
Re-install windows twice to repair instability
Flash the BIOS to repair instability
update drivers to be on the proper stable channels
Install a $30 dollar USB 3.0 card
Install the Xbox app to install the windows store, then uninstall the xbox app

Easy! But seriously, I would stay away from this computer if you're not a tinkerer or someone with experience troubleshooting PC and stability issues
reviews.author: BuddySettle

reviews.title: This PC was well worth it.
reviews.text: Product came faster than expected. Price tag was well worth it. The included keyboard and mouse was an amazing added bonus for the overall purchase. happy to have such a great PC for a fair price.
reviews.author: AidenBrothers

reviews.title: Awesome pc
reviews.text: Very satisfied, Great prebuilt. Would recommend to anyone trying to get a starter pc
reviews.author: AndrewL

reviews.title: Worst Buying Experience Ever
reviews.text: PC took a month and half to arive; constant delays. When it arrived the box was dinged and massively scratched and dirty, but thats just the box right? Wrong. PC was dinged itself. The bottom back screw/panel was bent to the point where it couldnt open it up.  Then finally, just like a previous review, the PC would not connect to the monitors. Incredibly frustrating. Had to return it because the “GPU was not functional”
reviews.author: Krezek

reviews.title: PC
reviews.text: Great PC , runs and looks amazing. Went back and forth on so many and I’m very satisfied with this one
reviews.author: DaevonJ

reviews.title: Dead Out of the Box
reviews.text:
The PC arrived dead out of the box, which means they didn't even test that it booted up before shipping it out. After spending almost $2,300 and waiting almost a month to get a PC, this is pretty abysmal.

Tech support for CyberPowerPC was helpful and it was determined that a defective motherboard is the likely cause of the issue.
reviews.author: Edward

reviews.title: great pc
reviews.text: i dont own this pc but overall my pc was 2000 and its amazing you may only need to upgrade the fans in general.
reviews.author: BeansS

reviews.title: CyberPowerPC
reviews.text: Good build.  Gigabyte Z690 motherboard, 32GB DDR5 RAM, Gigabyte Eagle RTX 3080, 1000 watt power supply.
reviews.author: Burx

reviews.title: Problems right out of the box
reviews.text: This was a Christmas presant and i cannot get the tower to connect to the monitor. I keep getting the message no single on the monitor. Yes i do have it connected to the correct ports which i tried the display ports and HDMI ports which i also made sure the cables were not damaged. I checked to make sure the correct imput source was selected. I also tried to connect it to another monitor and received the same message. I did check both monitors with a laptop and they connect with no problem so the monitors do work. I did check to make sure there are no loose or disconnected wires inside the tower due to shipping. I am very disappointed and I do have an appointment to have it looked at. I am still undecided if i will return this computer or wait to see what the tech says. If i wait until a tech looks at the tower and it is an easy fix i will update my review. I did do a search and i see this is a common issues with Cyberpower computers so i am very torn because who wants to pay money for something that you can’t use and may have continued issues.
reviews.author: Itiswhatitis

reviews.title: Very Pleased.
reviews.text:
Great build.
Good looking case and very clean & organized components.
I enjoy building PCs, but it would've cost more to do the same build if I bought the components myself. 
Never did RGB lighting in the past & have to say it's a fun addition.
My build came with a Gigabyte Z690 motherboard & 1000 watt power supply.
Gigabyte provides decent utilities if you feel the need to play with overclocking (I don't).
Received computer 5 days sooner than expected, day before Xmas (I'm in Texas).
Have not installed any games yet, but did some performance/benchmark tests & sure they will run great.

fyi.  If you get confused about setting up speakers, just search for Realtek Audio Console on your desktop.

Good job Cyberpower!

a
reviews.author: alanhecht

reviews.title: So far so good
reviews.text: It came with the specs it said it would and so far so good. Changing the rbg is a pain though there should be an easier way more options and I was a bit disappointed that the rtx 3080 was 10gb and not 12gb but asides from that it is really fast and effiecient.
reviews.author: John

reviews.title: Great machine
reviews.text: Excellent customized machine. Fast delivery and help.
reviews.author: WilliamW

reviews.title: Great Pre Built
reviews.text: My came with a 1000W power supply, Gigabyte RTX3080 12 gig, plenty of DDR5 Ram, and storage space aplenty for myself. No complaints. I was and still am a little nervous about the temps of the CPU under load (75-85C) but it's within spec for the 17-13700KF. I snagged this PC on a Black Friday special cheaper than I could build it.

reviews.title: Very good prebuilt
reviews.text: Really good pc, got 1000watts, z690 motherboard. 32gb gskill ram. only complaint is rgb fans are kinda hard to personalize but other than that its food.
